---
title: "R Code"
subtitle: "Annotated scripts from lecture, organized by topic."
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

Code used in class will be posted here. Download to your own computer for use in class

![](images/R.png){width="40%"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Setup: Installing R & RStudio

1.  Install R from [cran.r-project.org](https://cran.r-project.org)
2.  Install RStudio from [posit.co](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)



------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Day 1: Getting Started with R

Download these three files and put in their own folder. Assuming you have R and Rstudio installed, start by clicking on the "Project" file. 

[Week 1 Project](ClassCode/Day 1 - Intro/Week 1.Rproj){download="Week 1.Rproj"}


[Data](ClassCode/Day 1 - Intro/wbdata.RData){download="wbdata.RData"}


<a href="ClassCode/Day 1 - Intro/1 Intro.Rmd" download>Updated 1_Intro.Rmd</a>

## Day 2: Data Management and more R basics

Keeping it simple - Just one file today - download and put in your class folder

[Quarto day 2](ClassCode/Day 2 - Basics/Day2_Basics.qmd){download="Day2_Basics.qmd"}

<a href="ClassCode/Day 2 - Basics/Day2_Basics.qmd" download>Updated Day 2 Quarto</a>



*uses gapminder data so no need for rproject or dataset today


## Day 3: Getting to know your data

<a href="ClassCode/Day 3 - /Class 3 code.Rmd" download>Class 3 code</a>

[Pew Data](ClassCode/Day 3 - /PewData.csv){download="PewData.csv"}


## Day 4: Concepts and Cleaning

<a href="ClassCode/Day 4/4 Concepts and Cleaning.Rmd" download>Class 4</a>

<a href="ClassCode/Day 4/energsurv.xlsx" download>Energy Opinion Data</a>

[Energy Codebook](ClassCode/Day 4/Codebook.pdf){download="Codebook.pdf"}


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Day 5: R is my friend
<a href="ClassCode/Day 5/day5.Rmd" download>Class 5</a>

## Day 6: Plotting and Relationships
<a href="ClassCode/Day 6/Day 6.Rmd" download>Class 6</a>

<a href="ClassCode/Day 6/bech.RData" download>Bechdel Data</a>


## Day 7: Bivariate Regression
<a href="ClassCode/Day 7/7_bivariate.Rmd" download>Class 7 Regression</a>


## Day 9: Multivariate Regression
<a href="ClassCode/Day 9/9 multivariate.Rmd" download>Multivariate Regression</a>

<a href="ClassCode/Day 9/prem.RData" download>Prem Data</a>

<a href="ClassCode/Day 9/wages.csv" download> Wages </a>


## Day 10: More Regression and some Experiments

<a href="ClassCode/Day 10/10moreReg.Rmd" download>More Regression</a>


<a href="ClassCode/Day 10/10moreReg.Rmd" download>More Regression</a>
<a href="ClassCode/Day 10/HeightWage_MenWomenUS_HW.csv" download>HeightandWage</a>

<a href="ClassCode/Day 10/GerberGreenData.csv"download>GerberGreen</a>



<!--

[Class 3 Again](ClassCode/Day 3 - /Class 3 code.Rmd){download="Class 3 code"}

``` r
# ── Basics ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2 + 2          # arithmetic
sqrt(144)      # functions
x <- 42        # assignment
x * 2

# ── Vectors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
scores <- c(88, 92, 75, 98, 61, 84)
length(scores)
mean(scores)
sd(scores)
summary(scores)

# ── Loading data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(tidyverse)
data <- read_csv("data/survey.csv")
glimpse(data)
head(data)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Week 3–4: Descriptive Statistics & Visualization

[Download script](code/week03.R)

``` r
library(tidyverse)
data <- read_csv("data/survey.csv")

# ── Histogram ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(data, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# ── Boxplot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(data, aes(x = major, y = gpa, fill = major)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "GPA by Major", x = "Major", y = "GPA") +
  theme_minimal()

# ── Bar chart ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(data, aes(x = year)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "coral") +
  labs(title = "Students by Year", x = "Year", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# ── Numerical summaries by group ─────────────────────────────────────────
data |>
  group_by(major) |>
  summarise(
    n    = n(),
    mean = mean(gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(gpa,   na.rm = TRUE),
    med  = median(gpa, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Week 5–6: Probability & Distributions

[Download script](code/week05.R)

``` r
# ── Normal distribution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# P(X < 70) where X ~ N(75, 10)
pnorm(70, mean = 75, sd = 10)

# Find the 90th percentile
qnorm(0.90, mean = 75, sd = 10)

# ── Visualize normal curve ────────────────────────────────────────────────
x_vals <- seq(40, 110, length.out = 300)
y_vals <- dnorm(x_vals, mean = 75, sd = 10)
curve_df <- data.frame(x = x_vals, y = y_vals)

ggplot(curve_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_area(data = subset(curve_df, x < 70),
            fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "P(X < 70) shaded", x = "Score", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

# ── Binomial ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# P(X = 3) where X ~ Binom(10, 0.4)
dbinom(3, size = 10, prob = 0.4)

# P(X <= 5)
pbinom(5, size = 10, prob = 0.4)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Week 8–10: Confidence Intervals & Hypothesis Testing

[Download script](code/week08.R)

``` r
library(tidyverse)

# ── One-sample t-test & confidence interval ───────────────────────────────
scores <- c(72, 85, 91, 68, 79, 88, 74, 95, 83, 77)

t.test(scores, mu = 75, conf.level = 0.95)

# ── Two-sample t-test ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
group_a <- c(82, 88, 74, 91, 79, 85)
group_b <- c(70, 75, 68, 80, 72, 77)

t.test(group_a, group_b, var.equal = FALSE)

# ── Proportion test ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 54 successes out of 100 trials; test H0: p = 0.5
prop.test(x = 54, n = 100, p = 0.5)

# ── Chi-square test ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
observed <- matrix(c(30, 20, 25, 35, 15, 25), nrow = 2)
chisq.test(observed)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Week 13–14: Linear Regression

[Download script](code/week13.R)

``` r
library(tidyverse)
data <- read_csv("data/survey.csv")

# ── Scatterplot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(data, aes(x = study_hours, y = exam_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "coral") +
  labs(title = "Exam Score vs. Study Hours",
       x = "Hours Studied per Week", y = "Exam Score") +
  theme_minimal()

# ── Fit model ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
model <- lm(exam_score ~ study_hours, data = data)
summary(model)

# ── Diagnostic plots ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# ── Prediction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
new_data <- data.frame(study_hours = c(5, 10, 15))
predict(model, newdata = new_data, interval = "confidence")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: callout-tip
## Using These Scripts

Save each `.R` file in your project folder alongside your `data/` directory. Open in RStudio and run line-by-line with `Ctrl+Enter` (Windows/Linux) or `Cmd+Enter` (Mac).
:::

-->
